docs.title: Home
docs.text: Readme

docs.title: Vision
docs.text:
The aim of this project is to design a portable synchronous buck converter system that acts as a constant power source up to 90 W. Such a system should be powered by Li-on cells and be capable of generating an output voltage of up to 5 V and a current of up to 30 A. The system should dynamically adapt to the load, ensuring a stable output power set by the user. The system should have a number of safety features, such as:

  • overcurrent protection,
  • thermal protection,
  • protection against excessive cell discharge,
  • protection against incorrect cell polarity,
  • protection against excessively high or low load impedance,
  • graceful degradation of output power in not-ideal conditions.

docs.title: System requirements specification
docs.text:
Requirements specified include:

  1. Use Cases potential use cases for the product as a standalone device and daughter system for larger applications.

  2. Input Power Source Profile definition of input voltage range and characteristics of input source.

  3. Power Output & Dynamic Load Requirements definition on output power characteristics and range of loads.

  4. Mechanical & Environmental Constraints definition of size and approximation of durability in standalone version.

  5. User interface definition of power setting by user, both in standalone and subsystem versions.

  6. Safety & Protection Matrix definition of possible faults, their level of danger, and implemented protection protocols.

docs.title: 1. Use cases
docs.text:
This versatile High-Performance Low-Voltage Power Controller manages a wide range of applications, including:

  • Precision Resistive Load Driver Acts as a high-fidelity driver for various heating elements. The implemented Constant Power Mode ensures stable thermal output regardless of the element's resistance drift due to temperature coefficient (TCR). Applications include:

  • Portable Hot Wire Cutter,

  • High-Current Micro-Soldering Station,
  • Driver for Heated Wearable Technology,

  • Control system for Thermal Actuators.

  • Diagnostic and Validation Tool (PCB/Harness)

    Functions as a High Current Path Tester. Enables non-destructive stress testing of printed circuit boards power planes, connectors, and cable harnesses. Ideal for identifying high-resistance points, cold solder joints, and validating power delivery networks (PDN) under load.

  • Glow Plug Driver (RC/Automotive)

    Provides controlled low-voltage, high-current pulses suitable for driving glow plugs in RC models or auxiliary combustion engines, preventing element burnout through precise current limiting.

docs.title: 2.1 Physical characteristics of the source
docs.text:
Input source of this device in standalone version is 2X High-Current Li-Ion 18650 cells in series (Sony Murata VTC series or similar).

Description Min Typ Max Unit Input Voltage \(5.0\) \(7.2\) \(8.4\) \(V\) Input Current \(25\) \(35\) \(-\) \(A\) Internal Resistance \(-\) \(15\) \(20\) \(m \Omega\)

docs.title: Tests to verify input power requirements
docs.text: This document provides test procedures to verify requirements from REQ_INP_XXX group.

docs.title: TST_INP_001: Operating input voltage in Standby mode
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_001
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • Oscilloscope
  • SWD debugger

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Standby mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect probe to logic section input voltage and connect debugger.
  2. Set power supply to typical voltage: \(7.2\) \(V\).
  3. Check variables and voltage.
  4. Set power supply to min voltage: \(6.1\) \(V\).
  5. Check variables and voltage.
  6. Set power supply to max voltage: \(10\) \(V\).

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

standby_mode_flag is true AND logic section input voltage is approximately \(3.3~\text(V)\) in all checks

Fail

standby_mode_flag is false OR logic section input voltage is different then \(3.3~\text(V)\) in any of the checks

Info

docs.title: Test equipment

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect debugger.
  2. Set power supply to typical voltage: \(7.2\) \(V\).
  3. Check variables and current.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

standby_mode_flag is true AND Input current < [TBD] \(\mu A\).

Fail

standby_mode_flag is false OR Input current > [TBD] \(\mu A\).

docs.title: TST_INP_003: Active mode lockout in case of low input voltage
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_003
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Oscilloscope
  • SWD debugger
  • Programmable power supply

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Standby mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect probes to logic section input voltage and connect debugger.
  2. Set power supply between: \(6.0\) \(V\) and \(6.4\) \(V\).
  3. Attempt entering Active mode via user interface.
  4. Check variables and voltage.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

standby_mode_flag is set to true AND active_mode_flag is set to false AND logic input section input voltage is approximately \(3.3~\text(V)\)

Fail

standby_mode_flag is set to false OR active_mode_flag is set to true OR logic input section input voltage is different then \(3.3~\text(V)\)

docs.title: TST_INP_004: Active mode lockout in case of high temperature
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_004
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Oscilloscope
  • Hot air station
  • Thermal camera
  • SWD debugger
  • Programmable power supply

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Standby mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect probes to logic section input voltage and connect debugger.
  2. Set power supply to: \(7.2\) \(V\).
  3. Heat input section of the device between: \(95\) \(^{\circ}C\) and \(105\) \(^{\circ}C\).
  4. Attempt entering Active mode via user interface.
  5. Check variables and voltage.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

standby_mode_flag is set to true AND active_mode_flag is set to false AND logic section input voltage is approximately \(3.3~\text(V)\)

Fail

standby_mode_flag is set to false OR active_mode_flag is set to true OR logic section input voltage is different then \(3.3~\text(V)\)

docs.title: TST_INP_006: Deep Sleep mode activation in case of low input voltage
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_006
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Oscilloscope
  • SWD debugger
  • Programmable power supply

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Standby mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect probes to logic section input voltage and connect debugger.
  2. Set power supply to: \(7.2\) \(V\).
  3. Check variables and voltage.
  4. Set power supply between: \(5.1\) \(V\) and \(6.0\) \(V\).
  5. Check variables and voltage.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

  1. deep_sleep_mode_flag is set to false AND standby_mode_flag is set to true AND logic section input voltage is approximately \(3.3~\text(V)\) in first check
  2. deep_sleep_mode_flag is set to true AND standby_mode_flag is set to false AND logic section input voltage is approximately \(3.3~\text(V)\) in second check

Fail

  1. deep_sleep_mode_flag is set to true OR standby_mode_flag is set to false OR logic section input voltage is different then \(3.3~\text(V)\) in first check
  2. deep_sleep_mode_flag is set to false OR standby_mode_flag is set to true OR logic section input voltage is different then \(3.3~\text(V)\) in second check

docs.title: TST_INP_007: Auto Restricted mode activation in case of high source impedance
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_007
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • Electronic load
  • SWD debugger

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Standby mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Set power supply to: \(7.2\) \(V\).
  2. Set electronic load to: \(0.5~\text{m}\Omega\).
  3. Set output power of DUT to \(40~\text W\) via user interface.
  4. Set input impedance to less then: \(70\) \(m \Omega\).
  5. Attempt entering Active mode via user interface.
  6. Check variables.
  7. Set input impedance to more then: \(70\) \(m \Omega\).
  8. Check variables.
  9. Enter Standby mode via user interface.
  10. Check variables.
  11. Attempt entering Active mode via user interface.
  12. Check variables.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

  1. Variable active_mode_flag is set to true AND standby_mode_flag is set to false AND restricted_mode_flag is set to false in first check
  2. Variable active_mode_flag is set to false AND standby_mode_flag is set to false AND restricted_mode_flag is set to true in second check
  3. Variable active_mode_flag is set to false AND standby_mode_flag is set to true AND restricted_mode_flag is set to false in third check
  4. Variable active_mode_flag is set to false AND standby_mode_flag is set to false AND restricted_mode_flag is set to true in fourth check

Fail

  1. Variable active_mode_flag is set to false OR standby_mode_flag is set to true OR restricted_mode_flag is set to true in first check
  2. Variable active_mode_flag is set to true OR standby_mode_flag is set to true OR restricted_mode_flag is set to false in second check
  3. Variable active_mode_flag is set to true OR standby_mode_flag is set to false OR restricted_mode_flag is set to true in third check
  4. Variable active_mode_flag is set to true OR standby_mode_flag is set to true OR restricted_mode_flag is set to false in fourth check

docs.title: TST_INP_008: Auto Restricted mode activation in case of high temperature in input section
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_008
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • Hot air station
  • Thermal camera
  • SWD debugger

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Standby mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Set the power supply to \(7.2\) \(V\).
  2. Set output power to \(40~\text W\) via user interface.
  3. Attempt entering Active mode via user interface.
  4. Check variables.
  5. Heat input section between: \(70\) \(^{\circ}C\) and \(105\) \(^{\circ}C\).
  6. Check variables.
  7. Enter Standby mode via user interface.
  8. Make sure input section temperature is between: \(70\) \(^{\circ}C\) and \(105\) \(^{\circ}C\).
  9. Attempt entering Active mode via user interface.
  10. Check variables.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

  1. Variable active_mode_flag is set to true AND standby_mode_flag is set to false AND restricted_mode_flag is set to false in first check
  2. Variable active_mode_flag is set to false AND standby_mode_flag is set to false AND restricted_mode_flag is set to true in second check
  3. Variable active_mode_flag is set to false AND standby_mode_flag is set to true AND restricted_mode_flag is set to false in third check

Fail

  1. Variable active_mode_flag is set to false OR standby_mode_flag is set to true OR restricted_mode_flag is set to true in first check
  2. Variable active_mode_flag is set to true OR standby_mode_flag is set to true OR restricted_mode_flag is set to false in second check
  3. Variable active_mode_flag is set to true OR standby_mode_flag is set to false OR restricted_mode_flag is set to true in third check

docs.title: TST_INP_009: Operating input voltage in Deep Sleep mode
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_009
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • SWD debugger
  • Oscilloscope

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Deep Sleep mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect voltage probes to logic level input voltage and connect debugger.
  2. Set the power supply to \(7.2\) \(V\).
  3. Check voltage and variables.
  4. Set the power supply to \(5.1\) \(V\).
  5. Check voltage and variables.
  6. Set the power supply to \(10\) \(V\).
  7. Check voltage and variables.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

deep_sleep_mode_flag is true AND logic section input voltage is approximately \(3.3~\text(V)\) in all checks

Fail

deep_sleep_mode_flag is false OR logic section input voltage is different then \(3.3~\text(V)\) in any of the checks

Info

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

deep_sleep_mode_flag is true AND input current is less then [TBD]

Fail

deep_sleep_mode_flag is false OR input current is more then [TBD]

docs.title: TST_INP_012: Max input current in Active mode
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_012
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • SWD debugger
  • Electronic load

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Active mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect voltage probes to logic level input voltage and connect debugger.
  2. Connect electronic load to output and set it to \(0.2~\Omega\).
  3. Set output power to \(90~\text W\) via user interface.
  4. Set the power supply to \(7.2\) \(V\).
  5. Check current and variables.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

active_mode_flag is true AND input current is less then \(20\) \(A\)

Fail

active_mode_flag is false OR input current is more then \(20\) \(A\)

docs.title: TST_INP_013: Auto Standby mode entering from Active mode
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_013

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

  1. active_mode_flag is true AND standby_mode_flag is false in the first check
  2. active_mode_flag is false AND standby_mode_flag is true in the second check

Fail

  2. active_mode_flag is true OR standby_mode_flag is false in the second check

docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_017

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • SWD debugger
  • Hot air station
  • Thermal camera

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure equipment is at room temperature.
  2. Make sure input section of DUT has more then \(60\) \(^{\circ}C\).
  3. Configure DUT to enter Restricted mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect debugger.
  2. Set the power supply to \(7.2\) \(V\).
  3. Check variables.
  4. Cool input section of the device to less then \(60\) \(^{\circ}C\).
  5. Check variables.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

  1. restricted_mode_flag is true AND active_mode_flag is false in the first check
  2. restricted_mode_flag is false AND active_mode_flag is true in the second check

Fail

  1. restricted_mode_flag is false OR active_mode_flag is true in the first check
  2. restricted_mode_flag is true OR active_mode_flag is false in the second check

docs.title: TST_INP_018: Fail-Safe input continuous voltage without damage
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_018
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • SWD debugger
  • Oscilloscope

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text: 1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect voltage probes to logic level input voltage and connect debugger.
  2. Set the power supply to \(-30\) \(V\).
  3. Check input current.
  4. Set the power supply to \(30\) \(V\).
  5. Check input current.
  6. Set the power supply to \(7.2\) \(V\).
  7. Check voltage and variables.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

  1. Input current is below \(1~\text mA\) in first AND second check
  2. standby_mode_flag is true AND logic section input voltage is approximately \(3.3~\text(V)\) in third check

Fail

  1. Input current is more then \(1~\text mA\) in first OR second check
  2. standby_mode_flag is false OR logic section input voltage is approximately \(3.3~\text(V)\) in third check

docs.title: TST_INP_019: Auto Fail-Safe mode entering in case of input over-current
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_019
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • SWD debugger
  • Electronic load

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Standby mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect debugger.
  2. Connect electronic load to output of the input section and set it to less then \(25\) \(A\).
  3. Set the power supply to \(7.2\) \(V\).
  4. Check variables.
  5. Set electronic load to more then \(25\) \(A\).
  6. Check input current.
  7. Set the electronic load to less then \(25\) \(A\).
  8. Check variables.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

  1. standby_mode_flag is true in first check
  2. Input current is below \(1~\text mA\) in second check
  3. standby_mode_flag is true in third check

Fail

  1. standby_mode_flag is false in first check
  2. Input current is more then \(1~\text mA\) in second check
  3. standby_mode_flag is false in third check

docs.title: TST_INP_020: Auto Fail-Safe mode entering in case of over or under input voltage
docs.text:
Info

  • Traceability: REQ_INP_020
  • Type: Laboratory
  • Status: Draft

docs.title: Test equipment
docs.text:
• DUT
  • Programmable power supply
  • SWD debugger

docs.title: Test setup
docs.text:
1. Make sure DUT and equipment are at room temperature.
  2. Configure DUT to enter Standby mode.

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Connect debugger.
  2. Set the power supply to less then \(5\) \(V\).
  3. Check input current.
  4. Set the power supply to more then \(12\) \(V\).
  5. Check input current.
  6. Set the power supply to \(10\) \(V\).
  7. Check variables.

docs.title: Result criteria
docs.text:
Pass

  1. Input current is below \(1~\text mA\) in first AND second check
  2. standby_mode_flag is true in third check

Fail

  1. Input current is more then \(1~\text mA\) in first OR second check
  2. standby_mode_flag is false in third check
